description: American artistic gymnast and gymnastics coach (born 1958)
description_wiki: Barthold Wayne Conner (born March 28, 1958) is a retired American Olympic gymnast.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description_wiki: Norman () is the third-most populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma, with a population of 128,026 as of the 2020 census.

description_wiki: Paul Ziert is a former gymnastics coach.

description_wiki: Joplin is a city in Jasper and Newton counties in the U.S. state of Missouri.

description: town in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Locust Grove is a town in Mayes County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: public research university in Kansas, United States
description_wiki: The University of Kansas (KU) is a public research university with its main campus in Lawrence, Kansas, United States.

description: former aerospace manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: North American Aviation (NAA) was a major American aerospace manufacturer that designed and built several notable aircraft and spacecraft.

description_wiki: Rockwell International was a major American manufacturing conglomerate.

description_wiki: Portland Cement Association is a non-profit organization that promotes the use of cement and concrete.

description_wiki: The University of Oklahoma (OU) is a public research university in Norman, Oklahoma, United States.

description: American airline
description_wiki: United Airlines, Inc.

description: widebody airliner family (1968–2023)
description_wiki: The Boeing 747 is a long-range wide-body airliner designed and manufactured by Boeing Commercial Airplanes in the United States between 1968 and 2023.

surface_forms: next Olympics, which will be in Sochi, Russia
description: 22nd edition of Winter Olympics, in Sochi, Russia
description_wiki: The 2014 Winter Olympics, officially called the XXII Olympic Winter Games (Russian: XXII Олимпийские зимние игры, romanised: XXII Olimpiyskiye zimniye igry) and commonly known as Sochi 2014 (Сочи 2014), were an international winter multi-sport event that was held from 7 to 23 February 2014 in Sochi, Russia.

description: founder of modern Olympic Games, pedagogue and historian (1863-1937)
description_wiki: Charles Pierre de Frédy, Baron de Coubertin (French: [ʃaʁl pjɛʁ də fʁedi baʁɔ̃ də kubɛʁtɛ̃]; born Pierre de Frédy; 1 January 1863 – 2 September 1937), also known as Pierre de Coubertin and Baron de Coubertin, was a French educator and historian, co-founder of the International Olympic Committee (IOC), and its second president.

surface_forms: modern Olympics after the original Olympics
description: major international multi-sport event organised by International Olympic Committee
description_wiki: The modern Olympic Games (Olympics; French: Jeux olympiques) are the world's preeminent international sporting events.

description: 1947–1991 tension between the Soviet Union and the United States and their respective allies
description_wiki: The Cold War was a period of international geopolitical rivalry between the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR) and their respective allies, the capitalist Western Bloc and communist Eastern Bloc.

description: high school in Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Niles West High School (NWHS), officially Niles Township High School West, is a public four-year high school located in Skokie, Illinois, a north suburb of Chicago, in the United States.

description: village in Cook County, Illinois, United States
description_wiki: Skokie (; formerly Niles Center) is a village in Cook County, Illinois, United States.

surface_forms: first Olympics in Montreal the summer of ’76
description: Games of the XXI Olympiad, in Montréal, Canada
description_wiki: The 1976 Summer Olympics (French: Jeux olympiques d'été de 1976), officially known as the Games of the XXI Olympiad (French: Jeux de la XXIe Olympiade) and officially branded as Montreal 1976 (French: Montréal 1976), were an international multi-sport event held from July 17 to August 1, 1976, in Montreal, Quebec, Canada.

description: American college football coach, College Football Hall of Fame member (1871-1954)
description_wiki: Glenn Scobey Warner (April 5, 1871 – September 7, 1954), most commonly known as Pop Warner, was an American college football coach at various institutions who is responsible for several key aspects of the modern game.

description: hockey team of the National Hockey League
description_wiki: The Chicago Blackhawks (spelled Black Hawks until 1986, and known colloquially as the Hawks) are a professional ice hockey team based in Chicago.
